metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label and `land` command (deliverables of epic f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af) will exist before these docs ship, and add an explicit ordering dependency (e.g. 'blocked by f1fa Phase 1 stories') to the plan so the docs are never merged ahead of the infrastructure they describe.
findings.finding: AC1 instructs the rewritten docs to tell agents to 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`', but neither the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label nor a `land` command exist anywhere in the codebase (infra/gerrit/project.config has no Autosubmit label; no 'land' command appears in any source file). These are deliverables of the parent epic f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af, which is still `open` and unimplemented. The plan declares no dependency on that epic, so docs written under this plan would document non-existent infrastructure and deliver incorrect agent guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope or AC1: 'This plan is a child of epic f1fa; it must not be merged until the `Autosubmit` label and `land` command are live in production. Add a `depends_on` link to the implementing stories.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit prerequisite note (or sequencing AC) clarifying that the doc rewrite should only land AFTER the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label and `rebar land` command are deployed, OR scope the AC1 rewrite to the contract concept ('act on the single typed outcome') without hardcoding command names that don't yet exist.
findings.finding: The AC instructs that CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md be rewritten to tell agents to 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`', but neither the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label nor the `rebar land` command exists anywhere in the codebase (absent from infra/gerrit/project.config and from src/). An executor following this AC will write docs that reference non-existent infrastructure, producing a landing section that is immediately broken for any agent who tries to follow it.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a repo-wide doc sweep step (e.g., `grep -r 'green vote\|watch.*vote\|two.*vote' docs/ CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README*`) to locate and update or remove all stale references to the old vote-watching contract, not just the named landing sections.
findings.finding: The plan specifies rewriting only the 'landing section' of CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md, but does not include a sweep for other occurrences of the deprecated 'watch votes / two green votes' pattern that may exist elsewhere in repo docs (e.g., other sections of CLAUDE.md, README, inline comments, or other guides). Leaving stale references would contradict the new contract and silently reintroduce the broken behavior for agents reading those sections.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md landing section rewritten'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC or task: 'Audit all docs/ + CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md + README for occurrences of the old vote-watching pattern and update/remove them as part of this PR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the ADR-0042 template how the provider-seam deferral is cross-referenced (e.g., link to a GitHub issue, a future ADR stub number, or a named ideas doc) so the reference is resolvable by future maintainers.
findings.finding: The ADR-0042 AC references 'provider-seam deferred to idea 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984' — an informal identifier that may not resolve to a stable, discoverable document anchor. If this idea has no corresponding ADR, issue, or persistent doc, the ADR will contain a dangling cross-reference that future maintainers cannot follow.
findings.suggested_fix: In the ADR, write the deferral as: 'Provider-seam abstraction deferred — tracked in [Idea doc / Issue #XXX / future ADR-NNNN]' with a resolvable link.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/your-first-change.md` to the edit-target list (or explicitly scope it out with a rationale), since it encodes the same 'both votes green = it will land' model the plan is superseding.
findings.finding: `docs/your-first-change.md` teaches the old agent contract ('Repeat until both votes are green' at line 98; 'Once both votes are `+1`, a maintainer presses Submit' at lines 99–103) and is not listed as an edit target. It is cross-linked as the contributor on-ramp from CONTRIBUTING.md (line 28), README.md, and .github/pull_request_template.md — meaning any agent who follows the standard contributor path will still be taught the superseded 'two green votes = it will land' mental model even after this plan's edits ship.
findings.location: Scope section ('Docs + ADR only') / omission of docs/your-first-change.md
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/your-first-change.md §6 step 7, revise 'Repeat until both votes are green' and the Submit paragraph to match the new contract (e.g. reference the typed outcome / `land` command once those exist, or add a forward-pointer to CONTRIBUTING.md for the updated landing procedure).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require ADR-0042 to explicitly evaluate the Gerrit-native `autosubmit` plugin (used in Chromium/AOSP) as prior art — document why it was adopted/rejected/cannot be used — before committing to a custom implementation.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 (AC-2) is required to record 'the staged serial→batch plan,' but the plan does not require it to evaluate, acknowledge, or dismiss Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` plugin — which shares the exact label name, provides overlapping auto-submission functionality, and is used in production by Chromium/AOSP. Prior plan-review findings on three sibling stories all flagged this gap; none of the authored artifacts resolves it. An ADR that records a novel auto-lander without documenting why the native plugin was rejected (or why it cannot be used/extended) leaves the design justification incomplete.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-2: `docs/adr/0042-*.md` records the auto-lander
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC-2: 'ADR-0042 includes a prior-art section that evaluates the Gerrit-native `autosubmit` plugin (Chromium/AOSP) and documents why it was not adopted (e.g. plugin unavailability on self-hosted instance, agent-contract/outcome-enum requirements it cannot satisfy, or namespace collision rationale).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command per AC, e.g.: AC1: `grep -E 'Autosubmit|act on the single typed outcome' CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md && ! grep -iE 'two green votes' CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md`; AC2: `ls docs/adr/0042-*.md && grep -lE 'serial.*batch|outcome enum|separation-of-concerns' docs/adr/0042-*.md`; AC3: `grep -E 'ADR-0040|ADR-0041' CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md docs/adr/0042-*.md`.
findings.finding: The plan names no proving command for any of the three ACs. A docs-only plan still needs at least a grep/ls anchor per criterion so an executor can produce evidence of completion rather than relying on human reading.
findings.suggested_fix: Append a 'Verification' section to the plan with the three grep commands above, one per AC, so an executor can self-check without human prose review.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical source for the 'staged serial→batch plan' in AC2 (e.g. a linked ticket, an existing doc section, or a one-sentence summary inline) so an executor can document it without stopping to ask.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the ADR to record 'the staged serial→batch plan' but provides no pointer to where that design decision lives, leaving the executor to discover or invent the content.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to AC2: '...the staged serial→batch plan (see <link-or-summary>)...' or add a 'Background / Source Material' section listing the source doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description of the 'staged serial→batch plan' to the What or Why section so AC2's ADR content requirement is grounded in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the ADR to record 'the staged serial→batch plan' but the plan body (Why/What/Scope) never describes or explains what this plan is. An implementer has no basis from the plan text to know what to write.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('the staged serial→batch plan')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section, e.g.: 'The ADR should document the phased rollout strategy: initially the lander processes queued PRs serially (one at a time), with a later phase enabling batch parallelism once the gate substrate is proven stable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3 by replacing 'Docs' with the specific document(s) expected to carry the ADR-0040/ADR-0041 cross-references (e.g. 'ADR-0042', 'CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md', or both).
findings.finding: AC3 uses the term 'Docs' ambiguously — it is unclear whether ADR-0040/0041 cross-references belong in CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md, in ADR-0042, or both, whereas AC1 and the What section name the specific files separately.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Docs cross-reference ADR-0040 (FFO) + ADR-0041')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Docs cross-reference' in AC3 with 'ADR-0042 cross-references' if the intent is ADR-internal linkage, or 'CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and ADR-0042 each cross-reference' if all three should carry the links.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or ADR sub-requirement) that explicitly enumerates every member of the outcome enum and specifies the required agent action for each non-success outcome (at minimum: conflict, transient-failure/retry, and success).
findings.finding: The plan's AC instructs agents to 'act on the single typed outcome' and the ADR is required to record the 'outcome enum', but neither the plan nor any AC enumerates what those outcomes ARE or specifies what an agent must do for each non-success outcome (e.g., conflict, timeout, permission error, unknown failure). Docs that say 'act on the outcome' without specifying the outcome vocabulary and the required agent response for each case leave the non-happy-path handling under-specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'act on the single typed outcome'; ADR AC — 'outcome enum'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the ADR AC: 'outcome enum members are listed with their semantics and the required agent response for each: e.g., :ok → done, :conflict → rebase and re-submit, :timeout → retry, :error → report and stop.' Mirror a summary table or bullet list in the CONTRIBUTING.md landing section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a summary of the staged serial→batch delivery plan (Phase 1: serial auto-lander; Phase 2: batching) to the plan body (Why or What) so the ADR author has a specification to work from without relying on the parent epic.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the ADR to record 'the staged serial→batch plan', but the plan body (Why/What/Scope) never describes or explains what this plan is. The term originates in the parent epic f1fa's description but is not restated or summarized here. An ADR author working only from this plan has no specification to write the required ADR section.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'docs/adr/0042-*.md records … the staged serial→batch plan'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'The ADR should record the two-phase delivery: Phase 1 — a serial auto-lander (one change/chain at a time; no gate-semantics change); Phase 2 — cross-stack batching (assemble N non-conflicting changes into one CI run, land atomically).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require ADR-0042's AC to include a prior-art / alternatives-considered section that documents why established commit-queue tools (bors-ng, Mergify, GitHub native merge queue, Zuul serial mode) were not adopted for the serial→batch staged plan.
findings.finding: AC-2 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the staged serial→batch plan' as a design decision, but neither the plan nor the AC requires the ADR to justify this specific staged approach against established prior art (bors, Graphite, Zuul serial mode, GitHub's native merge queue). The parent epic calls the serial→batch staging 'bors' own evolution' without citation or comparative analysis. An ADR recording a novel architectural staging plan without documenting which alternatives were considered and why fails the 'novel pattern justified vs established approach' criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-2: 'the staged serial→batch plan'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
